generations.code:
But X doesn't have to fight everyone! X only needs to fight n-1 times total... wait no, X could fight 0 times if n=1, or potentially fewer battles.

Actually with n players and n-1 battles, exactly one player fights in every battle... no. Each battle eliminates one player. X needs to be the last one. X could potentially only fight once (beating the last remaining opponent).

So X can win if: there exists some player that X can beat (on some map), AND all other players can be eliminated among themselves.

Wait, but "all other players can be eliminated among themselves" - they need to fight each other. The game master can arrange this. Any set of players can fight each other as long as there are valid battles.

So: X can win if X can beat at least one other player (on some map). Because: have all others fight each other until one remains, then X fights that one.

But X needs to beat the final remaining player! So X needs to be able to beat whoever survives from the rest.

Let me reconsider: X can win if there exists a player Y such that X can beat Y on some map, AND all other players (except X and Y) can be eliminated by Y or by each other... this gets recursive.

Let me look at the examples. In example 1, only player 4 wins - they have max on both maps. In example 2, everyone wins.

I think the answer is: player X can win if and only if X can beat every player that X cannot avoid facing. X can avoid facing player Y if Y can be beaten by someone else first.

The correct condition seems to be: Player X can win iff for every player Y, X can beat Y on map1 OR X can beat Y on map2, OR Y can be eliminated by someone Z where Z can be eliminated too.

Let me try: X can win iff X can beat at least one player on some map, and recursively the "hardest" player X must face can be beaten.

After research/thinking: X can win iff X can beat the player who is strongest on map1 (among all others) OR X can beat the player strongest on map2.

The player with max a wins always. Player X can win if X can beat max_a player (needs b[X] > b[max_a_player]) OR can beat max_b player (needs a[X] > a[max_b_player]).

Let me verify with example 2: max_a is player 4 (a=21), max_b is player 1 (b=44). Player 1: b[1]=44 > b[4]=30 ✓. Player 2: b[2]=22 < b[4]=30, a[2]=12 < a[1]=11? No a[1]=11 < a[2]=12 ✓. Player 3: b[3]=11 < 30, a[3]=20 > 11 ✓. Player 4: max_a, wins ✓. All win!

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    if n == 1:
        print("1")
        continue
    max_a_idx = max(range(n), key=lambda i: a[i])
    max_b_idx = max(range(n), key=lambda i: b[i])
    res = []
    for i in range(n):
        if b[i] > b[max_a_idx] or a[i] > a[max_b_idx]:
            res.append('1')
        else:
            res.append('0')
    print(''.join(res))

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    max_a_idx = max(range(n), key=lambda i: a[i])
    max_b_idx = max(range(n), key=lambda i: b[i])
    
    res = []
    for i in range(n):
        can_beat_maxa = a[i] > a[max_a_idx] or b[i] > b[max_a_idx]
        can_beat_maxb = a[i] > a[max_b_idx] or b[i] > b[max_b_idx]
        if can_beat_maxa or can_beat_maxb:
            res.append('1')
        else:
            res.append('0')
    print(''.join(res))

Let me verify with example 2: max_a is player 4 (a=21), max_b is player 1 (b=44). Player 1: b[1]=44 > b[4]=30 ✓. Player 2: b[2]=22 < b[4]=30, a[2]=12 < a[1]=11? No a[1]=11 < a[2]=12 ✓. Player 3: b[3]=11 < 30, a[3]=20 > 11 ✓. Player 4: max_a, wins ✓. All win!

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    if n == 1:
        print("1")
        continue
    max_a_idx = max(range(n), key=lambda i: a[i])
    max_b_idx = max(range(n), key=lambda i: b[i])
    res = []
    for i in range(n):
        if b[i] > b[max_a_idx] or a[i] > a[max_b_idx]:
            res.append('1')
        else:
            res.append('0')
    print(''.join(res))

pipelines.final_code:
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    max_a_idx = max(range(n), key=lambda i: a[i])
    max_b_idx = max(range(n), key=lambda i: b[i])
    
    res = []
    for i in range(n):
        can_beat_maxa = a[i] > a[max_a_idx] or b[i] > b[max_a_idx]
        can_beat_maxb = a[i] > a[max_b_idx] or b[i] > b[max_b_idx]
        if can_beat_maxa or can_beat_maxb:
            res.append('1')
        else:
            res.append('0')
    print(''.join(res))
